### SEO-Optimized Description

**First 2 sentences (critical for search + suggested videos):**
> Your dentist may detect your heart attack risk before your cardiologist does. Dr. Victor Kane explains how bleeding gums create a bacterial highway to your arteries — and the 3 gum signals that predict cardiovascular risk months before a cardiac event.

**Full Description:**
```
Your dentist may detect your heart attack risk before your cardiologist does. Dr. Victor Kane explains how bleeding gums create a bacterial highway to your arteries — and the 3 gum signals that predict cardiovascular risk months before a cardiac event.

In this video, you'll discover:
• The oral-cardiac migration pathway — how P. gingivalis bacteria travel from gums to coronary plaque
• Why bleeding during flossing is NOT normal after 60 — it's an open-door mechanism for arterial colonization
• The 3 gum signals dentists notice first: recession pattern, bleeding location, and pocket depth
• Why standard cardiac tests miss what dental records reveal
• What to ask at your next dental cleaning to assess your heart risk
• The inflammation bridge connecting periodontal disease to endothelial dysfunction

This is NOT about replacing your cardiologist. Dental care is complementary to cardiac monitoring — and your mouth may be the earliest warning system you never knew you had.

📌 TIMESTAMPS:
0:00 — The authority reversal: dentist before cardiologist
1:15 — The open-door mechanism: bleeding gums as bacterial exit
2:30 — Arterial colonization journey: mouth to heart
4:00 — The 3 gum signals that predict cardiovascular risk
6:15 — Why dentists notice first (what they see that doctors don't)
7:45 — What your dental records reveal about your arteries
9:00 — Action steps for your next dental cleaning
10:30 — The inflammation bridge explained
12:00 — Empowerment close: your mouth is your early warning system

⚠️ DISCLAIMER: This video is for educational purposes only. Gum disease contributes to cardiovascular risk but does not "cause" heart attacks. Always follow your cardiologist's recommendations. Dental care complements — never replaces — medical care.

🔔 SUBSCRIBE for vascular health insights that connect the dots your specialists miss: [channel link]

📚 REFERENCES:
• Lockhart PB et al. (2012) — Periodontal disease and atherosclerotic vascular disease: does the evidence support an independent association? Circulation
• Schenkein HA & Loos BG (2013) — Inflammatory mechanisms linking periodontal diseases to cardiovascular diseases. J Clin Periodontol
• P. gingivalis in coronary plaque studies — Journal of Oral Microbiology

#DrVictorKane #GumDisease #HeartAttack #OralHealth #CardiovascularHealth #Over60 #DentalHealth #HeartDisease #PeriodontalDisease #ArterialHealth
```

### SEO-Optimized Description

**First 2 sentences (critical for search + suggested videos):**
> Standard cholesterol tests miss the one number that reveals how fast your arteries are ageing. Dr. Victor Kane explains GlycA — a blood test that measures arterial inflammation and predicts your "arterial age" decades before symptoms appear.

**Full Description:**
```
Standard cholesterol tests miss the one number that reveals how fast your arteries are ageing. Dr. Victor Kane explains GlycA — a blood test that measures arterial inflammation and predicts your "arterial age" decades before symptoms appear.

In this video, you'll discover:
• Why "normal" cholesterol doesn't mean healthy arteries — the inflammation gap standard tests miss
• What GlycA measures that LDL, HDL, and triglycerides cannot
• The "Arterial Age" framework — what the number means and how to interpret your results
• How to ask your doctor about GlycA testing (word-for-word script included)
• What to do with your results — lifestyle factors associated with lower arterial inflammation
• Why this test is NOT a replacement for standard panels — it's additional information

This video uses a real clinical case (details changed for privacy) to show how a patient with "perfect" cholesterol discovered their arteries were ageing 15 years faster than expected — and what they did about it.

📌 TIMESTAMPS:
0:00 — "I had a patient..." — the clinical narrative opening
1:30 — The standard test failure: when "normal" isn't enough
3:00 — What standard tests miss: the inflammation gap
4:30 — Introducing GlycA: the inflammation-specific biomarker
6:00 — The "Arterial Age" framework explained
7:30 — Number interpretation: what your result means
9:00 — How to get tested: the doctor conversation script
10:30 — What to do with your results
12:00 — Personal resolution close

⚠️ DISCLAIMER: GlycA testing is one option to discuss with your doctor — it is not universally recommended. This test provides additional information, not a diagnosis. Never stop prescribed medications based on GlycA results. Lifestyle changes may slow arterial inflammation progression — they do not "reverse" arterial aging.

🔔 SUBSCRIBE for vascular health insights that connect the dots your specialists miss: [channel link]

📚 REFERENCES:
• GlycA as biomarker of systemic inflammation — Clinical Chemistry
• Arterial age and cardiovascular risk prediction — European Heart Journal
• GlycA vs standard inflammatory markers — Journal of the American College of Cardiology
```
